---
title: "calc_wealth"
author: "Abidur Rahman"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.


## Description:
Computes the future value of an investment given an initial principal, ongoing monthly contributions, and a fixed annual rate of return. It simulates the compounded growth over time, returning a vector of balance values for each month.

**Usage**

calc_wealth(principal, monthly, rate, years)

Arguments:
principal = Initial lump sum investment.
monthly = Monthly contribution amount.
rate = Annual rate of return (as a decimal). Must be positive.
years = Investment duration in years.


## Value:
A numeric vector representing the investment balance at each monthly interval over the specified time period.

**Example**
```{r}
wealth <- calc_wealth(principal = 10000, monthly = 500, rate = 0.06, years = 30)
```
